forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a280
+project: kv-prefix-reuse
+title: 'Reorder review fan-out: diff+change in the shared prime, fork per rule'
+---
+## What
+Restructure the review fan-out so the LARGE content shared across the whole run (the change description + the file diffs) lives in ONE prime, and the small per-rule content fans out as forked suffixes. This maximizes the shared prefix and shrinks the divergent tail — which is what both backends reward: zero-rollback fork-restore on qwen (card w37g2tw) AND Claude's hosted prompt-prefix cache.
+
+Today (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`): the prime is per-VALIDATOR `render_validator_prefix` = `[shared sections + that validator's rules + PRIME_HANDOFF]` (`fleet.rs:335-345,658-666`), and the per-turn suffix is the file payload `render_file_payload` (`fleet.rs:671-678`). So the big diff is the per-turn suffix and is reprocessed for every validator, while the rules (smaller, per-validator) sit in the prefix. That's backwards for caching.
+
+Flip it:
+- Prime ONCE per review run with the maximal shared content: `[system + tools + change description + the file diffs under review]` (no rule-specific text). This is identical across all validators/rules.
+- Fork one child per rule (or per validator-rule), appending only `[rule text + output contract + any per-rule instruction]` as the small suffix.
+- Preserve the existing review semantics/output contract and the kanban GFM-checklist result; only the prompt assembly order and the prime/fork granularity change. Keep `pin_on_save` on the single shared prime.
+- Confirm this composes with verify-stage sessions (they can fork the same shared prime or stay independent — pick the option that maximizes reuse without changing verify behavior).
+
+This is backend-agnostic: it improves Claude caching immediately and is the workload that makes qwen fork-restore pay off.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] One shared prime per review run carrying change + diffs; rules fan out as forked suffixes.
+- [ ] Review findings/severity/dedup/kanban-checklist output unchanged vs current behavior.
+- [ ] Works for both backends (no backend-specific branching in the assembly).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-validators` asserting the assembled prime contains change+diffs and NOT rule-specific text, and each fan-out child appends exactly its rule suffix (assert prompt composition, not model output).
+- [ ] Existing review engine tests still green (`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators`).
+- [ ] clippy -D warnings clean.
+
+## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges currently dropped by a known bug): independently valuable for Claude; the qwen payoff requires fork-restore w37g2tw. Land after w37g2tw to test the qwen reuse end to end.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — assert the new prompt composition first, then refactor the assembly to produce it.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,29 +7,33 @@
 title: 'Reorder review fan-out: diff+change in the shared prime, fork per rule'
 ---
 ## What
-Restructure the review fan-out so the LARGE content shared across the whole run (the change description + the file diffs) lives in ONE prime, and the small per-rule content fans out as forked suffixes. This maximizes the shared prefix and shrinks the divergent tail — which is what both backends reward: zero-rollback fork-restore on qwen (card w37g2tw) AND Claude's hosted prompt-prefix cache.
+Restructure the review fan-out so the LARGE content shared across the whole run (change description + file diffs) lives in ONE prime, and small per-rule content fans out as forked suffixes. Maximizes the shared prefix and shrinks the divergent tail — rewards zero-rollback fork reuse on qwen AND Claude's hosted prompt-prefix cache (backend-agnostic win).

-Today (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`): the prime is per-VALIDATOR `render_validator_prefix` = `[shared sections + that validator's rules + PRIME_HANDOFF]` (`fleet.rs:335-345,658-666`), and the per-turn suffix is the file payload `render_file_payload` (`fleet.rs:671-678`). So the big diff is the per-turn suffix and is reprocessed for every validator, while the rules (smaller, per-validator) sit in the prefix. That's backwards for caching.
+Today (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`): prime is per-VALIDATOR `render_validator_prefix` = `[shared sections + that validator's rules + PRIME_HANDOFF]` (`fleet.rs:335-345,658-666`); per-turn suffix is the file payload `render_file_payload` batched by `batch_size` via `chunks()` in `run_validator_fleet` (`fleet.rs:231-310`). So the big diff is the suffix (reprocessed per validator) and rules are in the prefix — backwards for caching.

-Flip it:
-- Prime ONCE per review run with the maximal shared content: `[system + tools + change description + the file diffs under review]` (no rule-specific text). This is identical across all validators/rules.
-- Fork one child per rule (or per validator-rule), appending only `[rule text + output contract + any per-rule instruction]` as the small suffix.
-- Preserve the existing review semantics/output contract and the kanban GFM-checklist result; only the prompt assembly order and the prime/fork granularity change. Keep `pin_on_save` on the single shared prime.
-- Confirm this composes with verify-stage sessions (they can fork the same shared prime or stay independent — pick the option that maximizes reuse without changing verify behavior).
+Flip it, with these decisions PINNED (resolve the open choices the reviewer flagged):
+- **Prime once per review run** = `[system + tools + change description + all file diffs under review]`. No rule-specific text.
+- **Fan-out unit = per individual rule** (one forked child per rule), appending only `[rule text + output contract]`. This keeps rule isolation; do NOT fan out per validator-bundle.
+- **File batching goes away as a suffix dimension**: the diffs now live entirely in the prime, so `batch_size`/`chunks()` no longer chunks the suffix. Update/replace the batching tests accordingly — specifically `prefix_is_primed_once_per_validator_and_batches_fork_payload_only` and `fan_out_two_validators_two_files_submits_at_most_four_tasks` (these encode the OLD granularity and must be rewritten to the new "one prime + one fork per rule" shape). Consider the total prime size (all diffs at once) — if a change is very large this is a single big prime; that's acceptable and is exactly what we want cached, but note it.
+- **Verify stage (concrete, not an open choice)**: verify-stage sessions FORK THE SAME SHARED PRIME, with a test asserting they do. (`collect_forked_task` at `fleet.rs:476-537`.) If that's infeasible, the fallback is "verify stage unchanged" — but the default target is forking the shared prime for reuse.
+- **Empty-suffix guard**: ensure each per-rule suffix is non-empty (a rule always contributes text), so the fork turn never degenerates to `lcp == new_len` (full reprocess) — coordinate with w37g2tw's empty-suffix handling.

-This is backend-agnostic: it improves Claude caching immediately and is the workload that makes qwen fork-restore pay off.
+Backend-agnostic: no backend branching in the assembly. Preserve review semantics/output contract and the kanban GFM-checklist result.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] One shared prime per review run carrying change + diffs; rules fan out as forked suffixes.
-- [ ] Review findings/severity/dedup/kanban-checklist output unchanged vs current behavior.
-- [ ] Works for both backends (no backend-specific branching in the assembly).
+- [ ] One shared prime per run carrying change + all diffs; one forked child per rule appending only its rule suffix.
+- [ ] `batch_size`/file-chunk suffix batching removed; affected task-count/batching tests rewritten to the new shape (and named in the PR).
+- [ ] Verify-stage sessions fork the shared prime (test-asserted) — or documented as unchanged if proven infeasible.
+- [ ] Each per-rule suffix is non-empty.
+- [ ] Review findings / severity / dedup / kanban-checklist OUTPUT unchanged vs current behavior.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-validators` asserting the assembled prime contains change+diffs and NOT rule-specific text, and each fan-out child appends exactly its rule suffix (assert prompt composition, not model output).
-- [ ] Existing review engine tests still green (`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators`).
-- [ ] clippy -D warnings clean.
+- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-validators`: assembled prime contains change+diffs and NOT rule text; each fan-out child appends exactly one rule's suffix (assert composition, not model output).
+- [ ] Rewrite the two batching/task-count tests to the new "prime once + fork per rule" granularity.
+- [ ] Verify-stage fork test.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

-## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges currently dropped by a known bug): independently valuable for Claude; the qwen payoff requires fork-restore w37g2tw. Land after w37g2tw to test the qwen reuse end to end.
+## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges dropped by a known bug): independently valuable for Claude caching; the qwen end-to-end payoff requires fork-restore working (w37g2tw / 309wyrm). Land/measure after those.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — assert the new prompt composition first, then refactor the assembly to produce it.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — assert the new prompt composition + per-rule fan-out first, then refactor the assembly.
\ No newline at end of file
